When any growable plant is placed on Rich Soil, its growth will be randomly boosted, similar to the usage of Bone Meal.
Rich Soil can be tilled into Rich Farmland through the use of hoes. Rich Farmland is immune to mob trampling and won't unravel to Melons or Pumpkins, but requires hydration in order to boost crops.
Mushrooms planted on Rich Soil under sufficient darkness will eventually form a Mushroom Colony. Such colonies will grow from 2 to 5 mushroom caps over time, dropping them when harvested.
The decorative graves are special blocks with a fog particle only during the night.
The decorative graves can be haunted by a soul. The soul stays until used and allows some enchants like magic scroll/tablet, or upgrade the grave's key.

Rarely, a Grave Guardian can appear near a decorative grave. This guardian will protect the surroundings from hostile creatures and will offer to trade with the players. This is one way to obtain Grave's Dust and Essence of Undeath. During special events, the trades may differ.

&bMechanics:&r
&9Themes&r that you have discovered are listed in &eChronicle Of Echoes&r. At the moment, they are purely cosmetic and serve more to create an immersive effect.
&bExpertise Tiers:&r Your rewards scale every &e10 Expertise Levels&r. Every tier lowers the requirement for Rare loot.
&bTier 1+:&r Uncommon loot unlocked. You become eligible for the gold bonus (30%% chance). Further amount is scaled by your &eExpertise Level&r.
&bTier 3+:&r Rare loot unlocked.
&bThe "Grandmaster" Perk (Tier 50):&r If you ever reach Tier 50 (&eExpertise Level&r 500), the gold nuggets you receive as a bonus will transform into Gold Ingots.
&bThe Jackpot Slot (Tier 50+):&r You unlock a 5%% chance to trigger a &5legendary&r loot roll.
&9Themes&r
In simple terms, &9Themes&r are the categories that define what kind of loot you get from your &6Sealed Tome&r.

Instead of just getting "Zombie Loot" or "Skeleton Loot," the &5Echo Extractor&r analyzes the vibe of the block or spawner you are harvesting and classifies it into one of 31 distinct &9Themes&r (random process).
You have trapped the essence. Now you must break the seal to claim the rewards

&4Ropes&r are essentially reverse scaffolding: the can be continuously deployed downward from the same spot, and can be climbed without collision. This allows players to safely descend or ascend high altitudes, such as ravines or mountains.

When placing Rope, you can use more ropes against the first one to deploy them downward. This will continuously extend a column of rope down to the bottom-most Y-level possible, allowing for easy and controllable descents into chasms, ravines or from high locations.
To suppress rope deployment, simply sneak while placing it. If there's no space underneath to deploy, it will also place normally.

- Diodes can be placed in any direction so the most useful thing they can do is transfer Charge vertically upward
- Diodes can be pushed by pistons unlike the other components
- A Redstone clock is required to make the best use of a Diode as it only transfers 1 Charge when it receives a signal

Carved Nephrite is able to store experience inside itself in the form of "Charge" which it will equalize among neighboring Carved Nephrite. Charge will always prioritize flowing downward before checking to the side so keep that in mind when designing a storage vessel. Think of it like there's a fluid inside of the block. Each block of Carved Nephrite can store 100 Charge (just over 7 levels if you start from zero).
- &2&n&lNephrite Siphon&r&r&r
Nephrite Siphons are used to convert XP into Charge by draining XP from players that stand on it or absorbing nearby XP orbs
- &l&n&2Nephrite Fountain&r&r&r
Nephrite Fountains drain the Charge from Carved Nephrite it is placed on and converts it into XP orbs when it is powered with a redstone signal
If a Nephrite Fountain is adjacent to charged Carved Nephrite, it can be right-clicked with a glass bottle and the charge will be exhanged for a bottle of XP
- &l&n&2Nephrite Diode&r&r&r
- Nephrite Diodes take one charge from the Carved Nephrite behind it and transfer it to the Carved Nephrite in front of it whenever it receives a redstone signal

The &bMatter Recycler&r is a powerful tool used to deconstruct bulk materials into raw essence.
Use a &bRecycling Journal&r to track your progress. As your &bMastery Tier&r increases, you will nearly eliminate jams and significantly boost your &dJackpot&r rates.
&6How to Use:&r
• Place the item(s) you wish to recycle in your &bOffhand&r.
• Hold the Recycler in your &bMain Hand&r and &bRight-Click&r.
• Currently, this process is &bfree&r of XP costs, as the machine extracts energy from the items themselves.
&6Mechanics and Risks:&r
• &bGranular&r &bProcessing:&r The machine processes the entire stack at once, but calculates success for &bevery&r &bitem&r individually. One bad roll won't ruin the whole stack!
• &bJam&r &bChance:&r There is a base &b10%%&r &bchance&r per item to jam. If a jam occurs, the machine enters a &b10&r &bsecond&r &bcooldown&r to clear its internal gears.
• &dJackpot&r &dYields:&r Recycling &bDiamonds&r, &bNetherite&r, or &bGems&r can trigger a &dJackpot&r, awarding rare &bApotheosis&r &bGems&r or massive &bXP&r bursts.
&bThe Matter Recycler&r

Small Ships are an upgrade to vanilla boats. They're faster, more durable, and have more storage, at the cost of not being able to be picked up once placed. With this world's wide, endless rivers and oceans, that's hardly a problem. Sail the seven seas in your very own ship!

&6&lSurvival Tips
&e● Cup foods are emergency rations.
Miner's Delight cup soups give short effects and are
fast to eat mid-combat, but won't push you into MID tier.
Keep a few in your hotbar as a backup.
&b◆ Cook before you explore.
Raw and basic vanilla foods give almost no saturation.
Even a basic Farmer's Delight recipe makes a significant
difference in how long your buffs last.
&c✖ Don't enter dungeons starving.
Zero tier halts stamina recovery. ParCool moves and
combat rolls cost stamina - without regen, you'll be
locked out of movement after a few fights.
&a✦ Eat before a dungeon, not during.
Saturation drains from activity, not from time passing.
A HIGH-tier meal eaten just before entering will last
through most of a dungeon run.

Rich Soil is an enhanced type of dirt, more fertile and durable to the elements.
Rich Soil cannot be directly crafted. To make it, you must craft Organic Compost and place it into the world, letting it decompose over time.

Organic Compost will decompose more easily under sunlight and moisture from nearby water, but doesn't actually hydrate like farmland. It can also be gently accelerated by placing activators next to it, in a 3x3x3 area centered on itself.
Currently, the following blocks behave as activators for Organic Compost:
- Brown and Red Mushrooms, and their Colonies;
- Podzol and Mycelium;
- Other blocks of Organic Compost;
- Rich Soil, normal or tilled.

The &5Echo Extractor&r is a tool used to harvest Mob Spawners. Instead of breaking a spawner for a bit of XP, you are taking it's energy to create powerful &6Sealed Tomes&r which contain various loot.
&4The Ritual&r
- To use it, walk up to any Mob Spawner with &2Blessed Incense&r in your inventory and &bRight-Click&r.
- Success turns the spawner into Obsidian (draining it) and gives you a &6Sealed Tome&r. You gain &e+1 Expertise level&r.
- There is permanent 5%% chance to &4fail&r:
• &cSoft Failure&r (70%% of fails of those 5%%): The ritual fizzles out. You lose your incense, but the spawner is safe.
• &4Critical Failure&r (30%% of fails of those 5%%): The spawner turns into Obsidian.
- Low Extraction Expertise players also face a high &4Backfire Chance&r - 30%%, which summons Temporal Remnant. As &eExpertise Level&r increases, this risk fades down to 5%%.

ftbquests.chapter.dimensional_dungeons.q: First, here is a minimum raw materials list:
- 9x Stone Bricks
- 3x Chiseled Stone Bricks
- 24x Gold Ingots
- 1x Trapped Chest
- 1x Eye of Ender
Keep in mind you will also need to craft and activate keys. This is only the first step.
Creating the Basic Portal

A blank key cannot be used to enter a dungeon. A newly crafted key must first be used in an 'activation ritual' which will inscribe the name and location of a randomly generated dungeon onto the key. This action will require you to bring the key to a fitting location on The Overworld.
Basically, find a Stronghold, and use the key on the top of an End Portal Frame. You must aim for the black square on top.

Go back to your portal frame and place the key in the keyhole. If everything is correct then a golden dungeon portal should appear. If the frame was not built correctly then the portal will shatter immediately.

Keys can only be written to once. However keys are absolutely reusable! If you find a dungeon that is worth visiting more than once be sure to save that key somewhere in your base. A named key will always consistently take you back to the same dungeon.
Activating Portal Keys
Find a Key Inscribing Station

Dimensional Dungeons adds limitless procedurally generated dungeons to Minecraft! These dungeons are all placed in a single, separate dimension which can be accessed from a portal anywhere on the Overworld. This makes the dungeons themselves renewable and it provides challenging, replayable content.
The dungeon dimension limits building and block breaking. This allows for a proper adventure mode-like experience.

&cThe monsters in the dungeon are very strong, much stronger than any you would normally encounter.&r Their numbers, and your lack of options for dealing with them, make the dungeons very difficult. That is why the keys are so hard to craft! &cMake sure you're prepared for a tough fight before you enter one.&r

Besides challenging monsters, the dungeons are also full of worthwhile loot! Many rooms with valuable treasure chests require solving a puzzle that will test your knowledge of Minecraft game mechanics.
Find Advanced Portal Key in a dungeon
Second, combine one of your three Gilded Portal Blocks with the Trapped Chest and the Eye of Ender to form a Portal Keyhole Block.

Basically you need a frame of Stone Bricks surrounding two air blocks. And replace the top center block with the Portal Keyhole Block. The two spires on the side need to have your two remaining Gilded Portal BLocks on top.
Sinking the portal into the ground is a good idea so that you don't have to jump into it. But remember you still need to place the Stone Bricks on the bottom even if you can't see them!
Other Stone Brick variants can be used in the build, too. And the only air blocks that matter are the two blocks where the portal will appear.
{image:rad3:textures/misc/build_basic_1.png width:170 height:100 align:1}
Valid Portal Builds
Blank Portal Keys are crafted from a Blaze Rod and a Name Tag. You may want several.
Find all 8 unique trophies
Advanced dungeons are exactly what they sound like. Bigger layouts, tougher monsters, and improved loot!

While exploring basic dungeons you might find a Portal Keystone Block with an item in it. Take it! The item inside should be an advanced key, with a different naming convention and a different color. This is the only way to obtain advanced dungeon keys. They must be found!
Your basic portal frame is not powerful enough to handle these legendary new portal keys! You must enhance your basic portal with additional blocks.
First, you must hang two colorful banners, one on each spire. The banners should be white/purple with either color as the base. For reference, match the filled keyhole itself.
Second you must place two Portal Crown Blocks on top of your portal frame. You're going to have to rely on finding Portal Crown Blocks as loot in basic dungeon chests.
Find two portal crown blocks
Activate portal key
First craft three Gilded Portal Blocks, which are a Chiseled Stone Brick surrounded by 8 Gold Ingots.
Find a key to the teleporters hub
ftbquests.chapter.dimensional_dungeons.s: Limitless adventure from a single portal that you can build anywhere!
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest1B03: The &lDungeon Raid System&r is a custom roguelite layer built on top of the dungeon. Every run you enter, your performance is tracked — &lkills&r, &lchests opened&r, &lbosses slain&r, and &ltime survived&r all count toward your rewards.
&7/raidvault withdraw <amount> &r— move tokens to your inventory
&7/raidvault deposit &r— deposit held tokens back into the vault
At the end of each run you earn &6Raid Tokens&r, deposited automatically into your &6Raid Vault&r. Spend them here in this chapter to buy keys, items, and passes.
&lRank &r— the more you raid, the higher your rank. Higher rank means stronger mobs, but also a better token multiplier and access to greater rewards.
&lDeath&r — dying in the dungeon costs a portion of your current rank progress. You can never lose a rank from dying, but it slows you down.
&lRoom Events&r — once per run, a random modifier fires mid-run. It might buff you, curse the mobs, or make things considerably harder. Adapt.
&lCursed Chests&r — some chests are cursed. Opening them still gives loot, but also applies a debuff for a couple of minutes. Risk it.
&7/raidstats &r— view your rank, XP, and lifetime statistics
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest1E68: A key that hums faintly when held. &lEpic Lootboxes&r roll two times from a pool of high-value gear — diamonds, rare apotheosis materials, and dungeon-specific consumables.
Hold this key in your &loff-hand&r and &lright-click an Epic Lootbox&r to open it. Both the key and the box are consumed.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest2283: The rarest key. &lLegendary Lootboxes&r pull two times from the best loot pool in the system — greater experience scrolls, relic vouchers, powerful dungeon items, and a decent stack of Raid Tokens.
Hold this key in your &loff-hand&r and &lright-click a Legendary Lootbox&r to open it. Both the key and the box are consumed.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest29C1: A finely cut key with a blue sheen. &lRare Lootboxes&r contain noticeably better loot — experience scolls, card packs, ender perals and a chance at some Raid Tokens.
Hold this key in your &loff-hand&r and &lright-click a Rare Lootbox&r to open it. Both the key and the box are consumed.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest2AF3: A standard &lDungeon Pass&r. Use it on the dungeon portal to enter and start a new run.
The dungeon tracks your &lkills&r, &lchests&r, and &ltime&r from the moment you enter. Exit through the portal to collect your &6Raid Tokens&r and XP.
&7Tip: dying doesn't end your run — you can re-enter and keep going. Exiting the portal finalizes run.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest2F41: A selection of useful items for your next run. &lChoose one&r from the reward options — gear, consumables, or utility items to give you an edge in the dungeon.
Repeat this quest as many times as you can afford.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest30E1: A plain iron key. It won't open anything impressive, but &lCommon Lootboxes&r hold the basics — materials, food, and supplies that keep you going between runs.
Hold this key in your &loff-hand&r and &lright-click a Common Lootbox&r to open it. Both the key and the box are consumed.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest4552: A scroll dense with compressed experience. &lRight-click&r to absorb a large amount of XP instantly — useful for leveling enchantments or topping up before a tough run.
A reliable token sink if you have more raids behind you than ahead.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest6459: A concentrated strike capable of felling any dungeon &lBoss&r in a single use.
&lRight-click a Boss&r while inside the dungeon to instantly defeat it. The kill counts toward your run score and boss kill tracker.
&cSingle use. Only works inside the dungeon dimension.
ftbquests.chapter.dungeon_raid.quest7C1A: Channels enough force to dispatch any &lMiniboss&r in one hit. Comes with &l3 charges&r — each right-click on a valid target consumes one.
&lRight-click a Miniboss&r while inside the dungeon to use a charge. The kill counts toward your score and miniboss tracker.
For the ones that won't go quietly

Only recommended once you have a few ranks under your belt and solid gear. The dungeon does not scale down for the unprepared.

ftbquests.chapter.enchanting.quest1CA633: An Enchantment Library is a storage system for Enchantments. Simply insert enchanted books, and every enchantment on the book will be stored as points.
Each library has a Level Capacity, representing the highest level of enchantment it can store. Excess enchantments are voided.

Clicking on a stored enchantment will extract one level of that enchantment. Shift-clicking a stored enchantment will extract as many levels as possible. Books are generated in the output slot as necessary.
ftbquests.chapter.enchanting.quest2226FC: During the enchanting process, you spend &aexperience&r and &9Lapis Lazuli&r to enchant an item. From this, enchantments are randomly selected using some complex formulas. This quest explains that process.

First, we'll go over how enchantments are selected. Each enchantment has a  &dPower Function&r, which controls what &dFinal  power&r will make a specific level available. Once all the available enchantments are gathered, some are selected randomly based on their weights.

&dFinal Power&r is calculated using the following steps: Intially, you have a &6Base Power&r, which is the level you spent while enchanting. Then, &cQuanta&r and &eRectification&r modify your &6Base Power&r to produce your &dFinal power&r.

The specifics of how each stat modifies your &6Base Power&r can be found in the &cQuanta&r and &eRectification&r entries. Typically, a higher &dFinal Power&r will get you access to higher level enchantments.
ftbquests.chapter.enchanting.quest2365FE: This rune may be the best shelf in the pack, but it comes at a steep cost. But not to worry, the &5Nebula Cube&r can be obtained by any player, not just those bearing the &5Catastrophe Scroll&r! The &4Chaotic&r and &6Truth&r etchings do not need the player to have the Scroll. Go blow up some Withers!
ftbquests.chapter.enchanting.quest2830CB: The last step to Level 80 requires slaying the &0Warden&r, which allows crafting &1Sculkshelves&r.
ftbquests.chapter.enchanting.quest2D875F: Once you reach Level 45, you'll need to explore &5Infusion Enchanting&r to upgrade your shelves.
Create a few more of the type of shelf you were using, infuse them, and upgrade them. These new shelves, on top of your old ones, will let you reach Level 60.

ftbquests.chapter.enchanting.quest4A498C: With Apotheosis, enchanting is dependent on more than just levels. There are currently five new enchanting stats. Blocks that provide enchanting stats will have them on their tooltip.
By default, the rarities have the following weights:
Common: 10
Uncommon: 5
Rare: 3
Very Rare: 1
Every 10%% of &5Arcana&r changes the weights, eventually becoming fully inverted. Hovering the &5Arcana&r Bar will show the current weights.
&eRectification&r

&eRectification&r is a tertiary stat. It directly impacts the lower bound of &cQuanta&r modifications. Recall that your &4Min Power&r is equal to 1 - &cQuanta&r. With Rectification (R), your &4Min Power&r is equal to 1 - (1 - R) * &cQuanta&r.

If that doesn't make a ton of sense, don't worry about it. Just know that higher &eRectification&r values are always a good thing. You can also have &4negative&r &eRectification&r, which is always bad.
&8Clues&r
&aEterna&r
&8Clues&r are another tertiary stat. Each clue allows you to see one additional item in the preview window. When you can see every available enchantment, the text will change slightly.
You can also drop to zero clues, and be able to see nothing.
&aEterna&r is the primary stat of enchanting. Each point of &aEterna&r increases the maximum enchanting level by two. Some blocks can only provide &aEterna&r up to a certain maximum value.
&cQuanta&r

&cQuanta&r is a secondary stat. It controls enchanting variance. During enchanting, you have a &6Base Power&r, which is the level you spent to enchant with, and then a &5Final Power&r, after all modifications. &cQuanta&r is one of the factors in the modification process.
A value is selected, between 1 - &cQuanta&r and 1 + &cQuanta&r.

Your &5Final Power&r is equal to that value multiplied by your &6Base Power&r. Higher &cQuanta&r values increase the power range, which means your enchantments are less consistent in their &5Final Power&r.
&5Arcana&r

&5Arcana&r is a secondary stat. It controls the rarity weights, and at certain thresholds will guarantee you get additional enchantments. At 25%% &5Arcana&r, you will always receive at least two enchantments. At 75%%, you will always receive three.
Enchanting Stats

&n&5Better End Island&r&r

Obsidian pillars, End gateways, the spawn platform, and the portal itself in the middle of the island have all been redesigned. Additionally, the dragon no longer spawns from the start - instead, it will be summoned once a player gets close to the bell tower in the center.

&lTo resummon the dragon&r, the 4 crystals no longer go right next to the portal. Instead, you'll see 4 bedrock blocks on the floor inside the tower, one in each directional wing of the building. The crystals can be placed on these 4 blocks to start summoning the dragon again. That being said, we do actually still support vanilla crystal summoning positions, to avoid confusing players who aren't familiar with the mod.
&n&5Nullscape&r&r

Overhauls the End's generation in a way that maintains this identity - &0&oa realm of utter desolation&r&r. Nullscape will make you feel tiny and alone in the vast expanse of the End. You against the void - nothing more than that.

Nullscape boosts the End's height to 384 blocks, introduces a new terrain system based around materials science. Essentially, different regions of the End - on very large scales - have different properties and a different 'feel' to allow for almost infinite variation in terrain shapes. From sponge-like porous terrain to stretched alien landscapes to stacked rounded islands, you'll find new and interesting terrain no matter how far you go.
Mods: YUNG's Better End Island, Nullscape

Book Wyrms adds Book Wyrms to your forests, &2lizards&r that come in a variety of colors. You can herd them for their meat and colorful scales, using plain books to breed them. Most importantly, you can feed them &ounwanted enchanted books&r to &drecycle&r them into new random enchanted books.

Additionally, Wyrms have genes that can be improved (or worsened) and passed on to future generations. Use the &dBook Wyrm Analyzer&r to see these stats and breed them strategically to obtain your perfects specimens. The following stats can be meddled with:
- &9Enchanting level&r, determines how many levels the Wyrm needs to digest before making a new book (stronger enchantments are worth more levels) and how strong are the enchantments it can produce
- &2Digesting speed&r, determines how fast does the Wyrm digests books
- &eIndigestion chance&r, determines how likely the Wyrm is to have an indigestion and regurgitate some shredded paper and (magical) digestive fluids

While the bonuses produced by indigestion may feel bad, they can be refed to recover part of the magic of the digested books, or made into Wyrmutagen. This substance will let you modify the stats of your Wyrm's offsprings to a much better degree, if you can make enough of it.
Wyrms come in 7 colors, each with a different pool of enchantments they can provide:
- &8Gray&r, most common, can produce any enchantment
- &4Red&r, can produce enchantments for melee weapons like Swords
- &6Orange&r, can produce enchantments for ranged weapons, like Bows or Crossbows
- &aGreen&r, can produce enchantments for tools, like Pickaxes or Shovels
- &1Blue&r, can produce enchantments for armor or Shields
- &3Teal&r, can produce enchantments for Fishing Rods and Tridents
- &5Purple&r, can produce enchantments for some misc modded items

It's very important to store your experience so you don't lose it when you die. There are many ways to do so in this pack.

- &2Carved Nephrite&r can be used with Nephrite Siphons, Fountains, and Diodes to store experience in a physical structure in the world. Nephrite is also required for every other experience storage method. It can be found in geodes deep below the earth.
- &2XP Tomes&r can be used as a limited but cheap method of experience storage.
- The &2Tome of Peritia&r is an upgraded XP Tome which can store an unlimited amount of experience. However, it requires &4blood&r to create.

- &2Scrolls of Knowledge&r can store all your experience at once. However, not only do they require a grave soul, but they're also single-use. You can't store anything less than the full amount either. I don't recommend this one.

- The &2Experience Pump Upgrade&r works with a Tank Upgrade inside a backpack. It's easy to control, but the storage is limited by the storage space in the backpack. Using a higher tier backpack and stack upgrades can increase the storage size. Ultimately, I like this one the best.

- Piglins now have a reputation system that works similarly to Villagers. Doing things like fighting alongside them against Wither mobs, giving them gifts and bartering with them increases your reputation. Doing things like hurting or killing Piglin mobs and engaging in unauthorized mining/chest opening around them decreases your reputation.
- At +50 reputation with a Piglin, you will no longer be required to wear gold armor around them. At +100 reputation, you will be authorized to open chests and mine gold around them.
- At -50 reputation with a Piglin, you will no longer receive items from bartering. At -100 reputation, you will be attacked on sight regardless of whether or not you are wearing gold armor.

- Piglins seek out and pick up food items (by default, porkchops and cooked porkchops) and consume them, complete with an eating animation. Players can also right-click them with their food items if the Piglin has not already recently eaten and they will accept it. Eating food will also restore a Piglin's health in an amount identical to the food item's hunger value.

- Piglins can now barter with gold nuggets and gold blocks. Gold nuggets make you far more likely to receive common barter items, with all item stack sizes halved. Gold blocks make you far more likely to receive rare barter items, with all item stack sizes doubled.
- Piglins now shake their heads like Villagers when declining an offered item.
Piglins now emit particles/make sounds when accepting an item from you or declining it.
New Piglins Behavior

The Sempiternal Sanctums are 6 huge color-coded dungeons. Each one is filled with deadly guardians, traps, and puzzles. The loot is mostly in the form of netherite scraps or ingots. At the bottom of each Sanctum is a gate to a special challenge that rewards an Essence of the same color as the Sanctum.
I recommend bringing:
- some Windy Air to help reveal the Windy Air in the dungeon
- Depth Strider boots
- mobility: dashes, speed increase, extra jumps, maybe even flight

For the &4Red,&r &eYellow,&r and &9Blue&r challenges, I recommend using a few &6Hostility Orbs&r to remove hostility levels from the chunks that make up the room. Those challenges involve fighting, or at least surviving, against an onslaught of mobs, and removing hostility levels from those mobs will make those challenges a lot more doable.
After consuming the Essence of the Bees, enter a Sempiternal Sanctum

ftbquests.chapter.icaria.quest545FD79030: The kettle is a very confusing block. Here is a step by step guide on how to use it.
1. Fill the kettle with a bucket of Medeterranian Water.
2. Acquire a scroll from looting villages. Hold the scroll in your hand. Look at what ingredients it is asking for.
3. Gather each of those ingredients. Place them in the pot with right click.
4. When the last ingredient has been placed, wait a few seconds. Your finished spell will pop out of the Kettle.
ftbquests.chapter.icaria.quest552F10398C: The Forge is a powerful furnace used to smelt and alloy metals.
ftbquests.chapter.icaria.quest5E598ADB04: The mining progression of the mod is depth based, and vanilla tools aren’t very good in Icaria. While you can take your diamond pickaxe into the dimension to get started, it won’t help you get through the stronger stone types. From the top down, you will encounter these materials:
Yellowstone, Silkstone, Sunstone, Voidshale, and Baetyl.

Each stone type has its own subset of ores that can be found within. Yellowstone requires a Chert Pickaxe to mine, Silkstone requires a Chalkos Pickaxe, Sunstone requires an Orichalcum Pickaxe, Voidshale requires a Vanadiumsteel Pickaxe, and Baetyl requires a Sideros Pickaxe.
However, you can ignore all this in one of three ways:
a. The break spell from Ars Nouveau
c. Loot villages for better materials
If you choose the first two, I recommend also having a magnet so you don't lose items to the void.
ftbquests.chapter.icaria.quest60E551CDF4: This size of Titanite allows you to upgrade your weapons from +6 to +9.
ftbquests.chapter.icaria.quest64C5EB6BEB: Pots can be picked up and thrown to break them. They're full of loot!
ftbquests.chapter.icaria.quest6D5B912C32: Molybdenumsteel makes the best tools in Icaria, capable of mining any block.

ftbquests.chapter.icaria.questE28615A7A3: An airship to carry you from one island to the next! It takes any fuel that could go in a furnace.

ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest1359529A030017: Adds 3 more back slots. You can only wear one Triple-Strip Cape.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest14CF2EF91064A7: Resets the difficulty to minimum. It'll only raise from curios and dimension-specific difficulty. This curio lacks the downside of the Curse of Sloth, so you can still get special drops while you have it.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest1887FFFDF2F185: The first curse you should craft. It's required to progress further in the mod. Kill mobs for a small chance of obtaining the item form of a trait they have. These trait items are used in many crafting recipes.
This curse also raises the difficulty, allowing mobs with more powerful traits to appear. Make sure you're ready! Enchant your armor and weapons, and craft Detector Glasses if you haven't already.

Note: When an enchanted book appears in a crafting recipe, ANY ITEM with that enchantment can be used. So, for example, this curse can be crafted with a sword with looting and a pickaxe with silk touch.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest19E3721B38A7E8: These trait items are used to craft many advanced items. Any you don't need can be smelted into Miracle Powder, used in Miracle Ingots.
They can also be used on mobs to give them that item's trait. This includes some pets!
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest1BEBBCBDDF2D01: When an enchantment is applied to this book in an anvil, it creates two of that enchanted book. You'll need this later on.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest1EAB72EC26A866: This curse makes mobs drop Bottles of Curse based on their level. You are going to need A LOT of Bottles of Curse.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest22A646931FC362: Totemic gold tools and weapons heal you when they lose durability. They also deal more damage to undead.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest23F74C045B82BE: Unlock: Kill a mob with 10 traits
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest2447BA68A5E96A: Makes every mob as powerful as it can be for its level, but you'll always get all of its traits. High risk, high reward. Good for hunting down rare mob traits.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest28D8095FD4371A: This charm allows you to bypass the half-second of damage immunity that mobs get when struck. Very fast weapons can take advantage of this.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest2C7BE26B882BA9: Stops the difficulty from rising, but also stops you from getting special loot. Basically, a pause button on progress.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest2C8184EF506495: The Chaos Ingot is used to craft many powerful items. Curses, rings, charms, and more! You'll need a lot of these as you progress.

Note: Unlike the other rare drops, the Essence of Wind doesn't drop with the Magical Looting Charm equipped. You'll need to move 200m/s &othrough the air&r with Wind Capturing bottles in your inventory. Possible ways to achieve this:
- Shooting yourself out of a TNT cannon
- Getting knocked back by the Ender Dragon
- Holding a shield and getting rammed by a Ravager
- Casting Launch with a lot of Amplify on it
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest2F1B8FFAB949F4: Equipping the Chaotic Looting Charm enables even more unique drops.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest301FF317265D61: Poseidite weapons and tools are about as good as diamond on land. In the water, they receive huge boosts to speed and damage. They also deal extra damage to aquatic mobs and mobs damaged by water.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest32C34F16A99784: To make a Miracle Ingot, you need Hostility Essence. The only way to get that is by burning Bottles of Curse in fire or lava. It's a 1/256 chance per burned bottle. That means, to craft items that need Miracle Ingots or Hostility Essence, you're going to need A LOT of Bottles of Curse.
Bottles of Curse can also be drunk to increase your difficulty. This is the only way to encounter some of the rarest and most powerful mob traits, such as Ragnarok.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest36AF8F60D4EFD9: Adds 5 rings slots and 1 charm slot. Very helpful due to the sheer number of rings and charms in the pack.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest37EFC27EA5A642: Gives you more loot, but raises the difficulty. Raising the difficulty can be good, since it lets mobs with new, powerful traits spawn. High risk, high reward.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest39F87D8D27C272: Unlock: Kill a mob with 5 traits
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest424EC719533E15: Once you start seeing mobs above level 50, I highly recommend crafting and wearing a pair of these goggles. They'll stop invisible mobs from sneaking up on you. And they're just handy to have in general.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest43B44F0D48673B: A powerful offensive curse. Grants immunity to several negative effects and gives a massive, scaling attack boost. You'll definitely need it.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest44E782F20A5B4C: This curse makes mobs drop all their armor and weapons. Very helpful for collecting tridents or items with Protection 4.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest45806027148F0D: This item automatically unseals items that have been sealed by the Ragnarok trait. It is immune to being sealed.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest4647DFF4EABDE3: This looting charm enables unique drops from mobs based on their traits. These rare drops are far more difficult to get without this charm.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest4720DBBDF38D58: This anvil will never break and can be right clicked in the inventory to use it.
fun fact: it takes 558 anvils to craft this anvil
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest49999EA3508F42: Sculkium tools have high durability and break all blocks at the same speed.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest4AB1E47BD55C66: This charm is a must-have for all adventurers. Equipping it will allow mobs to drop rare items related to the traits they have. You'll want to hunt fiery, moonwalk, and gravity mobs for blaze powder and particles of lightning. Those are used to craft the next tier of looting charm.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest4B0874B6C46A93: Erases all potion effects, both good and bad. Also makes you immune to magic damage.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest4C9A4D021457F7: Stops you from getting obliterated in one hit. But watch out for the second hit.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest4D584EC8ED383E: An even more powerful offensive curse. Gives a scaling health and damage boost against mobs, but makes mob traits 300%% more common. Don't underestimate that downside.
I recommend removing this curse when summoning a boss, then equipping it again once it is summoned.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest500559C4EC48DA: Despite the similar-looking tooltip, the Abrahadabra is far more powerful than the Ring of Reflection. The Abrahadabra gives you immunity to (almost) all traits. Pulling, Repelling, Killer Aura, Ragnarok, they all do nothing to you. Reprint, though, still hurts.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest507B1A9A23B831: Unlock: Kill a mob with both Dispell and Dementor traits
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest544FFD8F98E83B: &2Regular Traits&r
- &9Moonwalk:&r mobs and players around it will have lower gravity, falling slower and jumping higher
- &3Counter Strike:&r when the mob is attacked, after it lands on ground, it will attempt to dash-strike back, launching itself toward target.
&n&4See more on the next page ->&r&r
&5Advanced Traits&r
&oThese traits only appear after Lv.100&r
- &3Adaptive:&r Mobs will get memorize and get used to damage types. Higher level allows mobs to memorize more damage types
- &9Reflect:&r Mobs will reflect direct physical damage. Try to use arrow or magic.
- &3Grenade:&r Shoot explosive bullet every few seconds after the previous bullet disappears.
- &9Growth:&r Slime only. Slime will grow larger when healed to full health. Growth III slime can be very large.
- &3Split:&r Mobs will split into 2 with half level and same trait (with 1 level lower split trait) when killed. Subsequent mobs won't drop anything.
- &3Fiery:&r Mobs can ignite you when attacked or attack you.
- &9Drain:&r Mobs will remove random positive potion effects, and increase duration of negative effects on target. Deals more damage if target has negative effects.
- &3Reprint:&r When hit target, copy target equipment enchantments. Increase damage based on target total enchantment points
- &9Corrosion:&r Appears only after Lv.200. When mob hit target, it will consume target equipment durability based on lost durability. Use Hardened enchantment to mitigate this effect.
- &3Erosion:&r Appears only after Lv.200. When mob hit target, it will consume target equipment durability based on remaining durability. Use Hardened enchantment to mitigate this effect.
&n&4See more on the next page ->&r&r
&ePotion Traits&r
- &5Blinder&r - Blindness
- &bDistorter&r - Nausea
- &5Levitater&r - Levitation
- &bStray&r - Slowness
- &9Invisible:&r Mobs and their armors/weapons will become invisible. Use Detector Glass to see them.
- &5Weakener&r - Weakness
- &bPoisonous&r - Poison
- &5Withering&r - Wither
- &bCursed&r (from L2Complements)
- &5Soul Burner&r (from L2Complements)
- &bFreezing&r (From L2Complements)
&n&4See more on the next page ->&r&r
&6Legendary Traits&r
&oThese traits will only be unlocked when you kills a mob with Rank 5 traits, and appear only after Lv.100&r
- &4Dementor:&r Mobs will be immune to physical attack, and their damage will bypass armor
- &3Protection:&r Mobs get damage resistance effect
- &6Dispelling:&r Mobs will be immune to magic attack, and their damage will bypass magical protections. They can also disable enchantments on your equipments for a short time
- &4Teleport:&r Mobs can teleport to avoid physical damage. Try to use magic.
- &6Repelling:&r Mobs will repel you. Use full set insulation enchantment to reduce the force.
- &4Pulling:&r Mobs will pull you toward it. Use full set insulation enchantment to reduce the force.
- &6Master:&r Mobs will summon allies. This trait is only found on special bosses.
- &4Killer Aura:&r Appears only after Lv.300. Mobs will deal damage to nearby targets every few seconds, range indicated by particle effects.
- &6Ragnarok:&r Appears only after Lv.600. When mob hit target, mob will "seal" some equipments or curios on target, which can be unsealed by hold using the item for a few seconds.
- &9Regenerate:&r Mobs will regenerate health slowly.
- &9Speedy:&r Mobs will move at absurd speed
- &3Tank:&r Mobs will gain much more health and armor
- &9Shulker:&r Shoot shulker bullets every few seconds. It will not inflict levitation, but will inflict - other potion effects the mob carries.
- &3Gravity:&r mobs and players around it will have higher gravity, falling faster and jumping lower
Mob Traits
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest5828F847AEC344: Shulkerate tools are extremely durable and have a slightly longer reach than most tools.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest5B05AD90640A64: Tired of getting blinded, weakened, poisoned, withered, nauseated, frozen, and slowed? This ring is for you!
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest5B44C59226BC1B: Straight-up multiplies mob drops. You can even get overstacked items with it. Even higher risk for even higher reward!
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest5C62136D3A02E4: Poseidite is made out of smelted tridents. The Curse of Lust is very helpful for making trident-wielding drowned always drop their tridents.

Poseidite armor is about as good as diamond armor on land. In the water, it becomes arguably the best armor in the pack, giving huge boosts to speed and defense. If you plan on wearing the full set DO NOT put Depth Strider on the boots. The Dolphin's Grace effect combined with Depth Strider will make it impossible to control your player in the water.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest5FDE0BD13FFD00: This book can be renamed in an anvil to the ID of any non-treasure enchantment (so no Mending or Vien Miner, for example) to turn it into that enchanted book. Can be very helpful, if expensive.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest638E809A1EB154: Equipping this allows you to bypass all protective traits and deal damage to the mob directly. Having this equipped is the ONLY way to kill a mob with both Dispell and Dementor. After you get your first one, any extras can be smelted down into chaos ingots.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest66DA3907930D74: Pairs well with the Ring of Divinity.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest6BB9F5B1E1AE4B: Miracle Ingots are used to craft the last two curses as well as other end-game gear.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest6DEAF842B0E407: Shulkerate can be crafted from iron, shulker shells, and shulker bullets in a bottle. It can also be looted from mobs with both Protected and Tanky as long as you have a Magical Looting Charm equipped.
Shulkerate armor is as good as diamond, but it has the Hardened enchantment built in, making it extremely durable.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest6DF2981982ED9B: The fourth and final looting charm. Equipping it will make Eternium nuggets drop from mobs with Ragnarok and the Imagine Breaker will drop from mobs with Dispell 3.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest774FA7C8E9128C: Totemic gold is obtained from smelting Totems of Undying. It can also be looted from mobs with Regenerating 3+ with a Chaotic Looting Charm.

Totemic gold armor is only about as good as iron, but it heals you when it loses durability. This pairs well with the Life-Mending enchantment. If you add the Hardened enchantment, you won't heal as much.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest78FABABB88DAE3: Sculkium can be crafted from silver, warden bone shards, and echo shards. It can also be looted from mobs with Tanky and Speedy 3+ with a Chaotic Looting Charm.

Sculkium armor has high defense, durability, and gives bonus health and attack speed. Wearing even one piece will give immunity to several negative effects and knockback. Wearing a full set will make you silent, undetectable by sculk sensors or the Warden.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest7B7FA58532DDA1: Heals you for 1%% of your health per second. Better than nothing, and you might be able to abuse it with really high health.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest7FAEDB31A2025A: Eternium can either be crafted one nugget at a time, or looted from mobs with Ragnarok if you have the Miraculous Looting Charm equipped. It is used to craft end-game gear.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.quest85E485E8091D54: - &4Mob strength&r progress with player. Player &nwould not&r encounter absurdly strong mobs at the beginning
- &4Mob strength&r are dependent on a lot of factors, instead of just being randomly strong.
- Reasonable progression: &cIncrease&r difficulty only when you kill strong enemies. &2Decrease&r difficulty when you die.
- There is a mechanic to make a region safe. You don't need to worry about strong mobs around your base
- 28 curio items to make you stronger and 2 enchantments to help fighting the mobs.

There is a &4level&r associated with all enemies. &4Level&r is determined by the &3dimension&r, &2biome&r, position, &6nearby player difficulty&r, and &centity type&r. As the &4level&r becomes higher, mobs have higher health and deal higher damage, and higher chance to have strong traits.
There is a &4difficulty level&r assigned to chunk sections and players.
&nKilling mobs&r to &ngain&r chunk and player difficulty, and &ndie&r to &nlose&r player difficulty.
Use &9Hostility Detector&r to know about current difficulty, use &5Hostility Spawner&r to make a chunk section permanently not spawning mobs with level.
ftbquests.chapter.l2.questA4A56A37F9F870: Pairs well with Totemic Gold equipment or the Hardened enchantment, but not both at once!

ftbquests.chapter.not_enough_food.quest1: The Cooking Pot is a workstation where you can both prepare and store a large amount of meals. When placed above any suitable heat source (campfire, open flames, lava, a stove etc.), it will start bubbling, indicating it's ready to cook.

You can put up to six ingredients inside a pot. If they match, the pot will start cooking them up into a meal. Most meals need a container to be served on, such as Bowls; the result slot will tell you which one to use. Simply add the container on the corresponding slot, or right-click the pot with it, to take a serving!

Meals tend to be quite large, so they don't stack up as high in your inventory. If you're not going to eat them all right away, you can just mine the whole pot and take it with you! Cooking Pots can hold 64 servings of any meal with a container, regardless of their maximum stack limit.
The Stove is a direct upgrade to the Campfire's cooking features, and a suitable heat source for the Cooking Pot.

To add items to its grill, simply right-click the Stove when it has no objects blocking its top face. Stoves detect campfire recipes to cook, meaning they usually also take 30 seconds to fully prepare items. Stoves cannot be automated through item transferring blocks (e.g. Hoppers), but require no fuel to cook items, much like a Campfire.
Stoves can hold six (6) items on its grill, and will gently pop cooked outputs above its surface, instead of throwing them around.
Stoves can be put out using shovels or buckets of water, and reignited with Flint and Steel.

Haste, Absorption, Luck, or rarer effects like Strength II.

Items can be placed on the Cutting Board by using them on it. It always prefers receiving items from the off-hand, allowing players to hold their ingredients there while holding the tool in their main hand, processing the stack simply by holding the use button.

When the correct tool is used on a valid item, it will be broken down into 1-4 different outputs, which are swiped sideways. Outputs always fly to the direction opposite to the Cutting Board's handle, allowing them to be collected by an item-capturing block, such as a Hopper or Basket. The tool receives 1 point of damage for each processed item.
The Cutting Board is a workstation where players can break down one item into many outputs, by using a valid tool on it.
ftbquests.chapter.not_enough_food.quest3: This combination allows your mount to be a lot more agile during travels. Since mounts eat a large volume of feed, the item does not need to be contained in a Bowl, saving some inventory space.

ftbquests.chapter.not_enough_food.quest6: Feasts are not edible directly as an item; they must be placed down as a block first. When placed, players can use (right-click) them with Bowls to serve themselves with a consumable portion.

Feasts are very large meals, which can be placed as a block to provide multiple servings to players.

ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest11AC8: As you descend into the darkness, the air grows cooler and mustier. You can hear faint sounds echoing through the tunnels, hinting at the presence of the bandits.

You're outnumbered, but you're not outmatched. You've honed your skills in countless battles, and they serve you well. You weave through the chaos, your senses heightened, your reflexes lightning-fast. You dispatch bandit after bandit, each fallen foe adding to your growing sense of triumph. But the fight is far from over. More bandits emerge from the shadows, their numbers seemingly endless.

The battle rages on, a brutal dance of death. You're surrounded, but you fight with the ferocity of a cornered beast. Your blade sings a deadly song, each strike a testament to your survival instinct.

ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest11B76: While Eldrin pores over the ancient fragments, he recalls a peculiar figure who might hold vital knowledge—the elusive Old Goblin Trader.

ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest13379: After some searching and perseverance, you finally uncover his hideout.

ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest13D89: You approach &8Silas&r and &9hand over the mysterious package&r. His eyes light up with gratitude, but there's a shadow of concern that crosses his face.
“&o&3Thank you for delivering this&r&r,” - he says, carefully unwrapping the package.
&o&8Inside, he finds a set of finely crafted tools, a much-needed addition to his workshop. But as he looks up at you, his expression turns serious.&r&r
“&3&oMy apprentice, Eli, went missing a few days ago,&r&r” - his voice heavy with worry.
“&3&oHe was last seen heading towards the Old Creeping Crypt. It’s a place filled with dark legends and eerie tales. Many say it’s haunted, and those who venture too close often don’t return.&r&r”

ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest14C73: You could &9open the package&r and see what lies within. &o(noone will find out about that)&r

ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest1575E: Village guard explains that the village is in dire need of supplies to prepare for the impending raid illager's gang. Your mission is clear: you must gather essential supplies to help fortify the village and protect its inhabitants.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest1667B: &o&8Inside: a sealed glass vial containing a rolled slip of parchment. A few old coins, green with salt. A folded cloth that might once have been a flag. And a smell — oak and sea air, somehow preserved across however many years this thing has been sitting in the dark.&r&r
The parchment is a page torn from a logbook. The handwriting is cramped and precise:

&6&o"— Trial the First is passed. If you are reading this, you walked through my crew and came out the other side. Good. They were the best I had. Don't feel too proud — they've been down there a long time and they're not what they were. But you showed up. Most people don't show up.&r&r
&6&o"The Blade is earned three times over. Find the maze. Find the sea. Find what's left of me. Then we'll talk."&r&r
&o&8No signature. Just the compass rose, drawn in the corner with a single stroke.&r&r
You pocket the fragment. The crate, now empty, feels lighter than it should. &8Like it's waiting to be filled again.&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest1DD4C: The log fragment mentions a maze. You've heard whispers of it — a dungeon that rearranges itself, or seems to. A place where &4monsters nest in impossible numbers&r and the walls are covered in the same kinds of glyphs you keep finding in Desram's wake.
&o&8The entrance, when you find it, is deliberately hidden. Someone went to trouble to conceal it. Or to preserve it.&r&r
Inside, the maze is exactly as described. Dark, loud, and alive.
&4Desram ran this alone. You try not to think about that too hard.&r
&o&8Somewhere in the deepest chamber, another crate. Same iron bands, same compass rose. Waiting, as if it expected you.&r&r
Clear the maze of its inhabitants
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest1EF12: As you wander through the charming streets of &2Willow Creek&r, the warm glow of lanterns illuminates the village, creating a cozy atmosphere.
&8&oThe scent of freshly baked bread and simmering stew wafts through the air, guiding you to the bustling village kitchen.&r&r
Inside, you meet &5Mira&r, the local cook, who is busy chopping vegetables and stirring pots. She looks up and smiles warmly.
&3“&oAh, a traveler! Would you be willing to help me prepare dinner for the villagers? I could use an extra pair of hands!”&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest2256D: You arrive at the designated meeting point.
But something feels off. The air feels thick, charged with an undercurrent of tension.
Bandits, their faces twisted in fury, charge from all around. They've been waiting for you, their ambush carefully planned.
You've been lured into a trap, a carefully orchestrated ambush by the very man you thought was your ally.

You're outnumbered, but you're not outmatched. You've honed your skills in countless battles, and they serve you well. You weave through the chaos, your senses heightened, your reflexes lightning-fast. You dispatch bandit after bandit, each fallen foe adding to your growing sense of triumph. But the fight is far from over. More bandits emerge from the shadows, their numbers seemingly endless.

The battle rages on, a brutal dance of death. You're surrounded, but you fight with the ferocity of a cornered beast. Your blade sings a deadly song, each strike a testament to your survival instinct.
You're not sure how much longer you can hold out, but you're determined to fight until your last breath.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest2A340: You crack the second crate open in the half-dark of the maze's deepest chamber.
&o&8Same ritual. Glass vial. Parchment. More of that impossible sea smell.&r&r
&6&o"— Trial the Second. You made it through the dark without a map and without much sense, I'd wager. Good. The maze doesn't respect preparation. It respects stubbornness.&r&r
&6&o"You want to know about the Blade. Everyone does. Here is what I can tell you:&r&r

&6&o"I forged it to pass on. Not because I was generous — I'm not — but because I understood something most people don't: a weapon that stays in one hand goes dead. It needs new blood. New fights. New proof that it's worth carrying.&r&r
&6&o"When it finds the right hand, it will know. When that hand proves itself, they'll know too. Then they pass it on, and the cycle holds.&r&r
&6&o"Lose it? Come back for another. The trials don't close. That's the whole point."&r&r
&o&8The second fragment slots against the first in your pack. You can feel the third out there — somewhere cold and wet.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest2F471: The third fragment is at sea. Or beneath it.

&o&8The map leads you to a coastline you don't recognise. The ship is a wreck, half-buried in sand and kelp, its mast snapped, its hull collapsed inward like a ribcage. The figurehead is gone but the prow still bears the serpent's coil around a compass rose.&r&r
&6This is Desram's ship.&r One of them, anyway.
&o&8The drowned that guard it are not like the ones in the crypt. Those were soldiers. These are sailors — the crew who went down with the ship and never came up. &r&r
&4The third crate is in the captain's quarters. Underwater. Sealed with something that isn't wax.&r
Collect nautilus shells from the wreck
Retrieve the sealed crate from the captain's quarters
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest303C9: As you approach the forest, a small, leather-wrapped bundle catches your eye at the foot of an ancient oak. Intrigued, you move closer.
A weathered note is tied to the package with twine. It reads:
&o&9For the Blacksmith of Willow Creek. Deliver with haste.&r&r

&o&8You recognize Willow Creek as a small village on the outskirts of the forest, home to a renowned blacksmith named Silas. You've heard tales of his skill, crafting weapons and armor of incredible strength.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest32996: At last, you find the &2Goblin Trader&r and press him for answers about the &emap fragments&r. However, he denies any promises, suggesting that it was likely one of his relatives who spoke of such things.
He reveals that many Goblin Traders roam both the Overworld and the Nether, each with their own secrets and wares.

&o&2"Promises? I don’t make promises lightly. Perhaps it was kin who spoke of such things. Know this - there are many like me, wandering both above and below. Seek them out if you wish to learn more."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest36768: A few days pass, filled with tense anticipation.
After you wake up in your camp you notice a small note without any text.
This will print coordinates to the chat. Write them down to not lose it.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest381F4: With determination in your heart, you decide to use the map to locate the bandits' hideout, which is said to be hidden deep underground. The clues you've gathered from the blacksmith, cook, and cleric guide you as you embark on this crucial mission.
The map indicates that the entrance to the hideout is concealed in a remote area, possibly covered by natural foliage or hidden within a rocky outcrop.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest38AF7: &o&8"These fragments... they look like parts of an old map. If we can piece them together, who knows what secrets they might reveal? You should speak with Eldrin, the Cartographer—he’s the best at deciphering such mysteries."&r&r
Meet the Cartographer NPC in nearest village
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest39939: While exploring and looting chests, you come across some &3worn and tattered paper fragments&r - remnants of what once was a map, lost to time. Intrigued by their potential, you decide to seek out someone knowledgeable who might help you piece together their meaning.
Perhaps the &6Cartographer&r or the &6Librarian&r in town can shed light on these ancient scraps.
I saw the village somewhere near...
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest3BB7A: You crack the third crate on the ship's deck as the tide comes in.
&o&8Same ritual. Vial. Parchment. But this one smells different — saltwater and something older underneath it, like air that hasn't moved in centuries.&r&r
&6&o"— Trial the Third. Good. You're wet, you're cold, and you're still here. That's the whole test, honestly. The maze and the crypt are just to thin the crowd.&r&r

&6&o"I'm not dead. I want to be clear about that. I'm somewhere further down than dead — past the roots of things, past where light gives up. But I can still hear it when someone passes my trials. I've been hearing it less and less. Good to hear it again.&r&r
&6&o"Bring the three pages together. Say my name at a place where the boundary is thin. I'll find you."&r&r

&o&8You look at the three fragments in your hand. Separated, they're scraps. Held together, they form half a map — the other half is in your memory now, assembled from the ruins and the water and the dark.&r&r
&4A place where the boundary is thin.&r You know what that means. Find somewhere the dead walk.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest3ED95: But the bandits are cunning and well-hidden. Bringing them to justice will require careful planning, strategic alliances, and a willingness to risk your life.
Will you be able to expose their hideout and secure their capture before they strike? The fate of the village rests on your shoulders.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest4011F: &0The atmosphere is thick with malevolence as you step inside. &r
&0Shadows dance along the walls, and the sound of distant moans echoes through the corridors. &r
&0Your mission is clear:&r &4confront the acolytes&r &0and extract the vital clues that will lead you to the source of the evil plaguing this place.&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest41E57: As you stand at the end of the &4Cultists' Crypt&r last corridor, a sense of urgency washes over you.

ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest424C3: The air hangs heavy with the stench of stale ale and unwashed bodies. You, a lone traveler, stumbled upon an &4Illager camp.
Overhearing their crude boasts, you learn of a brutal initiation rite: &na raid on the unsuspecting village&r.
&9A choice hangs heavy in the air, sharp as a brigand's blade...&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest440CE: He is not what you expected.

&o&8Not a skeleton. Not a spectre. Something harder to categorise — a shape made of sea-dark and old intention, wearing the outline of a man who was very good at being in charge of dangerous situations.&r&r
&6&o"You found the three pieces. You went where I sent you. Decent work."&r&r
He looks you over with eyes that don't quite exist.

&6&o"Now prove you can hold a blade. Not against my crew — they were a long time ago and they're not a fair test anymore. Against &4whatever's gathered here&r &6&osince the boundary thinned. If something is still standing after you're done, that's on you."&r&r
&4He's watching. He always watches the third part. It's his favourite.&r
Find a place where the boundary is thin
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest4737B: You could choose to follow the note's instructions and &9deliver the package&r to Blacksmith.
This would be the honorable thing to do, but it might mean missing out on whatever &5secrets&r the package holds.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest48BC4: &o&7"Ah, a keen eye and a brave heart! I am &6Eldrin, the Cartographer&r. &o&7I have long sought someone worthy to assist me in mapping the forgotten corners of this world. Will you accept the challenge?"&r&r
Eldrin provides you with a Pathfinder's Quill to reveal the location of the first structure. This site is a ruined watchtower rumored to hold clues to the cartographer’s greater mystery.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest4ADB9: Finally, through the smoke and dust, you see him: &4Krag&r, his scarred face twisted in a mask of rage. He charges, his blade a blur of steel. The clash is fierce, a whirlwind of blades and blood. You parry his attacks, your own blade finding its mark again and again. You fight with a ferocity born of desperation, fueled by a growing sense of vengeance.
In a final, desperate lunge, you disarm &4Krag&r, your blade finding its way to his heart. He collapses, his eyes wide with disbelief.

The fight is over. Silence descends upon the clearing, broken only by the ragged breaths of the survivors. You stand amidst the carnage, your heart pounding, your hands trembling. You've won, but at a terrible cost. You've slain Krag, the leader of the bandits, and claimed his sword, a symbol of your triumph and a reminder of the darkness you've embraced.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest4D1D0: As you speak with the cleric, he shares some intriguing rumors that could provide valuable insights into the bandits' activities and their plans.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest4DAF3: With the &6Nether Lily&r in hand, your quest to find the elusive &2Goblin Trader&r continues.
His movements remain unpredictable, and you must delve deep underground again to track him down.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest4DF38: &6&o"My spirit drifts further down these days. Toward the roots of things. Deeper than this world goes, almost. If you ever find your way to such depths — look for a compass rose carved into the dark."&r&r
&o&8Silence. The kind that follows violence — thick and particular.&r&r
Desram's shape recongeals from the shadows. He considers you for a long moment.
&6&o"Three trials. Three crates. Three fragments of something that most people would have given up on by now. And here you are, covered in whatever that last thing was, &nstill standing.&r&r"
&o&8He moves to stand in front of you.&r&r

&6&o"I name you bearer of the Questing Blade. Not owner — there are no owners. Bearer. It passes through hands, not into them. You hold it, you use it, you prove it was worth forging. Then you come back for another. That's the deal."&r&r
&o&8He extends one hand. The gesture is formal. Practiced. He's done this before — or tried to — and never got this far.&r&r

&6&o"The Wandering Hold will know your name. Bring it proof of a dungeon run — any run, one of the crates — and it'll give you a fresh blade. Lose it, break it, throw it into a volcano. Come back. The Hold endures."&r&r
Say Desram's name — let him find you
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest4F79C: The broker holds up a dark iron ingot.
&6&o"You've been in the graveyards. I can tell. This metal comes out of the same places Desram's crew guard."&r&r
&o&8He sets it on the scales. It weighs more than it should.&r&r

&6&o"Desram called it the &nspectral tithe.&r&6&o The undead don't spend coin, but they carry things from the world they came from. Things the living can use. He said it was the only honest tax he ever collected — the dead don't haggle."&r&r
&o&8The broker adds a second entry to the ledger. A standing arrangement, below the others — smaller print, like it was added as an afterthought.&r&r
&6&o"Bring me what the dead leave behind. I'll give you what the living need."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest51F2F: From somewhere that doesn't quite exist, a sword arrives.

&o&8It looks like a sword. Feels like one too — solid, balanced, made to be used rather than admired. But there's something in the grip that isn't quite weight. A sense of accumulation. Every hand that's held this, every trial it's witnessed. It remembers.&r&r
&6&o"When you lose it — and you will — you know where to find me."&r&r
&o&8The voice comes from nowhere and everywhere at once. You look up. Desram is gone. &r&r
In your pack: the Blade. In the world: more dungeons, more crates, more proof to gather.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest52B28: Join the bandits and raid a village.
This will print coordinates to the chat. Write them down to not lose it.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest548F6: Prove your worth by participating in the raid.

The villagers are not defenseless, and failure could mean a swift and brutal end.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest5826E: As you delve deeper into the &0Old Creeping Crypt&r, the air grows colder, and the shadows seem to whisper secrets of the past.

&4Your heart races&r as you navigate through the damp, stone corridors, finally stumbling upon a hidden chamber. In the flickering light of your torch, you discover a crumpled note lying on a dusty altar.
Unfolding the note, you read Eli's hurried scrawl:

&o&3"If you find this, know that I have embraced the truth. The artifact we seek lies within the Cultists' Crypt. Follow the path of the moonlight to the ancient oak, and there you will find the entrance hidden beneath the roots."&r&r
This will print coordinates to the chat. Write them down to not lose it.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest5B0D5: A chilling wind sweeps through the air, carrying with it the faint whispers of the undead.
You start digging and eventually the entrance looms before you, &0dark and foreboding&r, with the faint glow of eerie green light flickering from within.
You steel yourself for the challenges ahead, knowing that the &4crypt&r is teeming with &0undead&r &4corrupted creatures&r that will stop at nothing to protect their secrets.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest5D522: &o&8Having successfully completed the raid, you stand amidst the spoils, the adrenaline still coursing through your veins. The air is thick with the scent of smoke and fear, a stark testament to the night's events. You carefully gather yourself, the weight of your actions settling upon your shoulders.&r&r

Before you lies the next step: contacting &4Krag, the leader of illagers&r to claim your place within their ranks. You carefully craft a message, using charcoal and a scrap of parchment salvaged from the village.
&o&3As promised, I have proven my worth.
&o&3I await your instructions regarding the location of your hideout.
&o&3I am ready to join your ranks.&r&r

&o&8You carefully fold the note, concealing it within a small, hollowed-out stone. This will be your messenger, a silent testament to your newfound allegiance. You choose a discreet location near the outskirts of the bandit camp, placing the stone in a pre-determined spot known only to the bandits. &r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest5F0EF: The notice from the tavern board mentioned it: &6The Wandering Hold.&r

&o&8Now you know what it is. Not a place — or not exactly. A system Desram built before he vanished, to keep the cycle moving. A set of standing arrangements with brokers, traders, and old contacts who owe him more than they'd like to admit.&r&r

Bring them an &6iron-banded crate&r — proof of a dungeon run, proof you've kept working — and they'll give you a new blade. Or resources. Or other things, depending on what you bring and who's buying.
&o&8The brokers ask no questions. They check the compass rose stamp on the crate, check it again, and deal. That's all Desram ever asked of anyone: do the work, deal straight, move on.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest62729: The brokers of the &6Wandering Hold&r deal in dungeon-proof.

&o&8The iron-banded crates — stamped with Desram's compass rose, found in crypts and mazes and drowned holds — are the currency. The brokers don't care what was inside. The stamp means someone went somewhere dangerous and came back. That's what they're buying.&r&r
What they sell in return depends on what you bring and which arrangement you invoke.
&8The standing deals are listed above. Some are visible from the start. Others open up as your history with the Hold deepens.&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest638B3: &6&o"Lost it? Good. That means you used it."&r&r
&o&8The broker behind the counter doesn't look up when you slide the crate across. He checks the stamp, runs his thumb across the compass rose, nods once.&r&r
&6&o"Desram's standing order. A crate for a blade. The arrangement endures."&r&r
&o&8He reaches below the counter and produces a sword that looks exactly like the one you lost. Same weight. Same feel in the grip. Same sense of accumulated history.&r&r
&4It's not the same sword. It never is. That's the whole point.&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest63D38: Now that I helped the village to prepare I also gathered enough evidences to locate illager's hideout.
Some of the townfolks also decided to join me.
This will print coordinates to the chat. Write them down to not lose it.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest6585D: Not everyone comes to the Hold for a blade.

&o&8Some come for the other side of the arrangement — the spoils. Dungeon runs yield proof; proof yields coin; coin yields whatever the Hold has on the shelf. The brokers are pragmatic. They don't ask what you need it for.&r&r
Slide the crate across. Take what's offered. Leave.
&8The arrangement is simple. The Hold prefers simple.&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest70B13: As you assist the Blacksmith, he shares valuable clues about the bandit hideout that could prove crucial for your mission.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest7155A: The Hold's brokers deal in proof. But they also deal in &4other things.&r

&o&8Contraband — goods moved without manifests, through routes that don't appear on any harbour-master's ledger. The Hold has always had a side arrangement for this. Desram built it in from the start.&r&r
&6&o"We ask no questions about where it came from. You ask no questions about where it's going."&r&r
&o&8The broker slides a blank receipt across the counter. No names. No cargo description. Just the Hold's watermark and a space for a thumb-press.&r&r
Move the contraband through the Hold
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest734E1: Left with no clear answers and the fragments still in your possession, you feel overwhelmed. The path forward is uncertain, but one thing becomes clear: the &6fragments&r hold value when traded with knowledgeable Cartographers, elusive Goblin Traders and maybe someone else?
Perhaps this is the key to unlocking their true power.
&o&8"Though the road has been winding and fraught with dead ends, the fragments are not without worth. Your next step lies in forging alliances and trading wisely."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest73B68: &9You decide to&r follow the note's instructions and &9deliver the package&r to Blacksmith.
&6&oHonesty and a sense of duty guide you. &r&r
However, the path to &2Willow Creek&r is not without its challenges. &lGather your supplies first!&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest75F8C: After your work with the blacksmith, you turn your attention to the cook, who shares some intriguing rumors that could provide further insight into the bandits' activities.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest76A7E: A new entry appears in the Hold's ledger, written in ink that smells faintly of something &5deep and mineral.&r
&o&8The broker looks at it, then at you, with an expression that approaches surprise.&r&r
&6&o"This arrangement was placed by the King himself. A long time ago. It was marked 'dormant until someone brings proof they've been below.' Below meaning &nwell below&r&6&o."&r&r
&o&8He taps the coin on the counter. Turns it over.&r&r
&6&o"Undergarden coin. Can't be counterfeited — the metal doesn't exist anywhere else. Desram left instructions for what to give in exchange."&r&r
&6&o"He said you'd know what to do with it."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest777B5: The broker slides a second ledger page across the counter.
&6&o"You've been busy. The Hold notices when someone runs the same dungeons more than once. Most people do it once and consider themselves done. You apparently consider yourself &nnot done.&r&r"
&o&8He stamps a new entry in the ledger.&r&r
&6&o"Three crates at once. Better rate. Desram's arrangement — he called it the 'bulk discount', which is a peculiar term for a pirate king to use but he had a sense of humour about commerce."&r&r
&o&8The broker allows himself a very small smile.&r&r
&6&o"He left notes on a lot of people. You're in them now."&r&r
Submit three dungeon crates at once
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest7A72C: After clearing all the undead in the Tower I returned with nothing.
&6Eldrin &rtasks me to gather some stuff for his further researches...
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest7BA2C: Take some time to gather additional supplies and gear before heading into the unknown.
