ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest7C9B2: The &2Old Goblin Trader&r is a cunning and secretive figure who rarely stays in one place for long.
His lair lies deep beneath the surface, hidden in the twisting tunnels of the underground. His location shifts frequently, making him pretty difficult to find.

&o&8"The Goblin Trader is a slippery one, always moving his den to avoid unwanted visitors. It took all your wit and patience to track him down, but now that you’ve found him, the real challenge begins."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest7EA78: A message appears at the Hold's counter, sealed with wax and no name:
&4&o"If someone matching your description has been making trouble for the Pillager captains, we have an arrangement to discuss."&r&r

&o&8The broker explains, somewhat reluctantly: there was a debt. An old one, between Desram and a line of Pillager captains who used the same contraband routes back when the King was still operational. They owed him passage and never paid. The Hold's been waiting on it for years.&r&r
&6&o"We can't collect ourselves. We're brokers, not soldiers. But if you're already putting Pillager captains in the ground, there's an arrangement in that."&r&r
&o&8He pushes a small ledger across. An entry for &4'recovered debt — banners received as proof.'&r&r
&6&o"The King was patient about the debt. He said eventually the right person would come along. Apparently that's you."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest7FE6E: Report the bandits to the guards in the nearby town.
This will print coordinates to the chat. Write them down to not lose it.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.quest859A9: You ask around. Sailors, merchants, a half-deaf cartographer. Slowly, a picture forms.

&o&8Born in a fishing village with no name, he took his first ship at seventeen, his first fleet at twenty-three. He never claimed a crown — he said crowns were for people who needed others to know they won. But the Shattered Coves obeyed him. Every captain, every smuggler, every dock master who moved contraband through the southern routes did so under his shadow.&r&r
What made him legendary was not the conquests. It was the &6Questing Blade.&r
&o&8He forged it himself, they say. Not with a blacksmith's craft, but with &4three trials&r &o&8— dungeons he ran alone to prove he was worth the steel. And when it was done, he made a rule: &r&r
&6the sword was never to be kept. Only carried. When its bearer proved themselves, they would pass it on. The blade would find the next hand that deserved it.&r
He disappeared before he could pass it on himself.
&o&8The fragments of his story are scattered across the same dungeons he once cleared. Find them, and you may find him.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.questB0881: Now, you wait, the anticipation hanging heavy in the air.
The path you've chosen is fraught with danger and uncertainty, but the allure of power and belonging is strong.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.questE8BBA: The crypt opens beneath a hill that locals refuse to name.
&o&8Inside, the air smells of brine and iron — not the usual rot of old graves, but something that remembers the sea.
&4Not as men. As whatever men become when they swore an oath to a king who never died and never came back.&r
&o&8They don't attack out of hatred. They attack because that is what the oath demands. Guard the trial. Turn back the unworthy. Let through only those the Blade might choose.&r&r
Among the fallen, you find something unexpected: a small iron-banded crate. Whatever is inside, it was meant to be found.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.questED99B: Pinned to the notice board, half-covered by a missing goat poster, is a notice written in faded red ink.
&o&3"Any soul with knowledge of the final resting place of &n&4Desram the Unbroken&r&r&3&o, once &6King of the Shattered Coves&r, &3is sought by the order of the Quill and Anchor.&r

&o&8You've heard the name before. Every sailor has. Desram was a pirate king who vanished without dying — no shipwreck, no battle, no grave. Just gone. His sword, they say, went with him. And his sword, they say, chooses its bearer.&r&r
The notice gives no address. Only a name: &6The Wandering Hold.&r
The dungeons stir. Something now waits inside.
ftbquests.chapter.sidestories.title: Side Stories
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest12: The Paladin is a Fighter with the power of Light and healing magic, as well as defensive buffs for their allies. Most paladins got their powers from their devotion to gods but paladins do not nesessary follow or worship deities to gain power. Some paladins got their powers throught sacred oaths and virtues. Their dedication to their faith gives them various prayers, healing abilities and holy-based spells to protect themselves and others. Naturally, they are a type of Magic Knight. They are also quite good at laying the smite down on undead, demons and other traditionally evil supernatural creatures.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest15: Barbarian  is a character who throws themself into a fight with such reckless abandon, it seems like they want to die. It could be over-enthusiasm, overconfidence, Unstoppable Rage, or the desire to die in battle. Whatever the cause, it's usually accompanied by a bellowing warcry. Sometimes with total obliviousness to whether they're actually fighting the enemy. And they never, ever retreat.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest17: The Thief, when it is a separate class, is a version of the Rogue with lower damage, but the ability to steal items from enemies. Sometimes, this can extend even to intangible items, such as experience points, but more commonly includes rare items that cannot be obtained in any other way.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest21: Fighter puts emphasis on strength and physical combat. Wielding melee weapons, they typically lack range, or have only limited ranged attack options, but they excel at fighting enemies in close range combat, often having high HP, defense, and attack capabilities to aid in this. In a team or party based game they put themselves at risk at the front of the group and take the brunt of the damage meant for their less sturdy comrades. A Fighter's strength is boosted by the wide variety of equipment they can use and they are usually able to equip the widest variety of weaponry and armor, including stuff too heavy for other classes. Most of the time the Fighter is considered the default "hero" class and as such they are often considered good for beginners.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest34: The Warlord is a tactical master. They can hold their own in frontline combat as well as giving out buffs to their underlings and allies, usually by commanding them to superior positions than the ones they would have thought of on their own, and they may have protective auras made of their own charisma to increase a team's effectiveness in battle. Sometimes may be able to summon their own troops.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest3A: Archers are skilled bowmen. Generally independent and used to working alone, this tends to make them practical, a little haughty or arrogant and not the best at following orders, especially from those they don't respect. Due to the nature of their weapon, archers are typically stealthy and graceful and tend to fight at a distance. As a result, they're often portrayed as vulnerable to close-quarters combat unless they switch to a different weapon.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest40: An offensively-focused spellcaster who lacks some of the weaknesses of their setting's normal Squishy Wizards - they're tougher and can cast their spells faster or more reliably, in exchange for their arsenal being weaker or limited entirely to offense. Explanations for this can vary from special training, to special equipment, to their magic being somehow "artificial". Usually has at least a mild military theme, being either a soldier (possibly a Super Soldier) or someone who Had to Be Sharp. Tends to use spells with shorter ranges than other spellcasters, or to simply cast their spells at close range more often. Can sometimes equip better weapons or armor than other mages, in which case they're distinguished from the Magic Knight through poor physical stats or lack of advanced combat skills.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest56: The Dark Knight class is the opposite number of the Paladin, possessing the power of Darkness and often necromantic or dark-magic oriented abilities that deal large amounts of damage to enemies, although often at the cost of their own HP. They may have abilities that lower their target's stats or inflict status effects also. As the opposite of the Paladin, they may do more damage to light based enemies such as angels, fairies or other traditionally good/holy creatures even if Light Is Not Good or find such foes to be their biggest weakness.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest59: A squishy dedicated healer with little abilities at offense, though their magic may be able to harm specific types of enemies, most commonly demonic entities and the undead.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest5A: Pirate will use a combination of weaponry instead of specializing - usually being able to switch freely between pistols and swords. They may also receive bonuses for fighting in water terrain or on ships, or be the best class at swimming. May have some aspects of the Bandit, or the Bandit class may be folded into Pirate entirely.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest64: While they have all the standard Rogue abilities, they tend to use them in less subtle ways - instead of unlocking a door they'll kick it down, and instead of stealthily pickpocketing their enemies they'll simply walk up and grab things from them. Sometimes they also have the ability to intimidate their enemies or are good at destroying structures, as well as whole slew of other "gang" related abilities.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest6F: A more offensive Rogue, who sacrifices technical expertise for better stealth and killing abilities. Often have a variety of weakening and poisoning abilities and are able to cripple a foe to leave him open for allies or to let him die from Damage Over Time.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest75: Pet Masters specialize in controlling a powerful "pet" to fight by their side. The pet can be anything from a tamed animal to a summoned elemental to a constructed Golem, but usually it serves as a front-line melee combatant while the master hangs back to support it with healing, buffing and long-range attacks. In extreme cases the master does not take part in combat at all. If the pet is an animal then this class is often folded into Ranger or Nature Magician, and comes with the ability to control animals in general. If folded into Summoner then the character might have access to multiple pets, but only be able to use one at a time.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest7D: Kits are sets of items that are designed to help you at the start and throughout your gameplay.
You can see them as classes or professions but they mostly do not affect your gameplay(doesn't add new skills or stats) and served purely for roleplaying purpose.
Some of them are aquired from quests and some are buyable for coins in shop.
First tiers of kits will offer simple general items and starting from second tier you will get Artifacts associated with the name of the kit.
Choosing your first Specialization is free and to aquire tier 2 and 3 Specialization you will need to do some tasks.
ftbquests.chapter.specialization.quest7F: Rogues are a diverse class that contains everything from dexterous thieves, to treasure-hunters, to assassins. Masters of stealth and infiltration, Rogues prefer to attack when their opponent is least aware. They are quick but fragile, unable to take heavy damage, and instead rely on their speed to dodge attacks and slip in and out of the shadows to catch their opponent off guard; setting traps and inflicting status effects on foes to give them the edge. Outside of battle Rogues usually have a wide range of helpful utility abilities such as lockpicking to allow them to open locked doors and treasure chests without needing to find/buy keys or the ability to detect and disarm traps. Some can even steal items directly from enemies allowing the player to obtain items that otherwise might be unobtainable. Sometimes Rogues are lumped in with the Ranger class, but more commonly they are differentiated by specializing in melee - particularly with light blades and daggers or ranged attacks using throwing weapons such as knives and shuriken.

ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest2BFD9A8BD602B: The passage down from the settlement is wide and well-cut — or was. The dwarven lanterns have guttered out over the centuries.

&o&8Your torches cast long shadows on walls that grow more elaborate the deeper you go. The geometric patterns have become something richer: scenes. Histories. A whole civilisation illustrated in bas-relief — dwarves founding their first forge, expanding their tunnels, discovering something vast and bright deep in the rock. The Vein, surely. In the early carvings they are celebrating it. Later, they are kneeling before it. Later still, they are running from it.&r&r
The tunnel narrows. You hear something ahead — not the Vein, but something smaller.
&2Scuffling. Muttering. The clink of things being handled carelessly.&r
Goblins. Of course there are goblins.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest304845FA014A1: It is enormous. Carved from a single piece of amber the size of a house, its surface cracked with age but still pulsing with a dim inner light.
&4Seated upon it — or rather, fused with it — is a figure that was once human. The ancient king.&r
&o&8Power radiates from the throne in waves. You could feel it from across the room. Whatever is stored inside this thing — centuries of offering, sacrifice, worship — it could be yours.&r&r
The only thing between you and it is whatever remains of the king.
&4Incompatible with "Bind the Throne — Restore the Seal"&r
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest34DE8CE324CBA: When equipped in a &bCurio slot&r, this ring acts as your 'Soul Insurance.'
Higher ranks (from &7Wanderer&r to &dLegend&r) grant significantly better &bRandomized Kits&r upon every rebirth. Your progress is announced to the world!
&oUse &b/rank&r to check your points and milestones at any time.&r
- Upon &4death,&r the ring will &bshatter&r, clearing your inventory.
- You are &bresurrected&r at a random location with a &bStarter Kit&r based on your &6Rank.
- Your &bPoints&r and &6Rank&r are permanently bound to your soul.
Earn &2Progression Points&r by conquering the world:
- Having specific &5powerful item&r in your inventory
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest3587F64719C50: Not cleanly — not like Maren's ritual would have bound it. It tears apart, its essence scattering through the trees in ribbons of violet smoke. The corrupted hollow shudders and goes silent.

&o&8The curse lifts. Not gently — it leaves all at once, like a held breath finally released. Your senses return in a rush: the smell of pine and wet earth, the sound of wind in the canopy overhead, the faint warmth of sunlight filtering through the branches.&r&r
You're alive. The spirit is gone.
Behind you, Maren steps out of the trees. For the first time, she looks something other than composed.
&5&o"...And it worked. I didn't think it would work."&r&r
She hands you something — not gratitude, she's incapable of that — but something more useful.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest35B611772CD81: Medallion in hand, you press deeper.
The passage eventually opens into a vast underground chamber — the &6Deep Works.&r

&o&8The scale is staggering. Forge-towers rise from the cavern floor to the distant ceiling, connected by walkways and ramps. Every surface is covered in carved symbols, but here they are inlaid with dark metal, and they still faintly &4glow&r. At the chamber's far end, a great sealed door — easily fifteen metres tall — bears the Forge-Masters' emblem enlarged a hundredfold.&r&r
And in front of it, arranged in a semicircle, are the dwarves.

&o&8They are not moving. Not dead, not exactly — preserved in a state between rest and stone. Their faces are peaceful. Each one faces the door. Each one holds a tool. They finished the job. They sealed The Vein. And then they stayed to make sure it held.&r&r
&4The door hums. The Vein, behind it, is still singing.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest35EDEA3061D1A: The longer you stand in the Vein's presence, the more you understand.

&o&8Not in words — the Vein doesn't think in words. But in impressions. You see the dwarves finding it, millennia ago, and the joy of it. You see them building their civilisation around its warmth. You see the moment, generations later, when they realised the warmth was &4changing&r — pulling at them, drawing them inward, making it harder and harder to remember the surface.&r&r
&4The Vein was not trying to harm them. It simply wanted company.&r

&o&8But the Forge-Masters understood: something that cannot comprehend harm is still capable of causing it. So they built the seal. They stayed to hold it. And they sent the medallion away with a courier who never arrived.&r&r
You pry a single fragment from the vein wall. The crystal sings against your palm — a clear, high note, satisfied.
The door reseals behind you as you leave.
&o&8The dwarves are still at their posts. You think — you aren't certain — that one of them is smiling now.&r&r
Take a fragment of the Vein
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest35FED99D71DA1: You drop into a mineshaft — a routine descent, torches already placed by whoever dug here before you.
But thirty blocks down, the wooden supports stop. The tunnel walls change: no longer rough-hewn dirt, but &6dressed stone&r, carved with a precision no modern miner could match.

&o&8Someone else was here first. Long before the mineshaft. Long before anyone living can remember. The stonework is immaculate — each block fitted without mortar, smooth as glass. Geometric symbols run along the ceiling in a repeating pattern, worn but still legible to someone who knows what to look for.&r&r
You don't know what to look for. But you know this didn't come from nothing.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest375994C478ED2: The core of the mod is its mechanics, which add new ways to attack and defend. These are intended to supplement the vanilla combat system, and make new playstyles viable. If you aren't sure how to get started, a patchouli guidebook is available, crafted with a book and gold sword.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest38049609F73B8: Mobs now have a chance to spawn as Champions, elite mobs with enhanced stats, extra abilities, and extra loot (Enchanted Books and Coins).
Champions have affixes that grant them powers, with higher tiers having more affixes.
There are currently 6 tiers of Champions, locked behind game stages.
For information on what coins which tiers drop, check out the Coin Shop!
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest383426FBF6B0B: Following the direction the merchant came from, you find the first signs of ancient civilisation:
&6Sandstone columns&r, half-buried. Carved reliefs showing figures bowing to a great seated form — a ruler, crowned in amber, surrounded by kneeling armies.

&o&8The ruins stretch further than you expected. A civilisation must have thrived here once, before the desert swallowed everything. But why did they carve their king's image on every surface with such reverence? And why, in every depiction, do the worshippers have their eyes closed — as if they dared not look directly at the throne?&r&r
The ruins are crawling with undead. &4They shouldn't be this far from a crypt.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest394D7906EC60F: A weathered merchant collapses at the edge of a desert trading post, barely alive.
Before losing consciousness, he presses a cracked clay tablet into your hands.
&6&o"The Amber Throne... don't let them wake it... the seal is broken..."&r&r
&o&8The tablet is covered in glyphs you've never seen before. The merchant is beyond help — whatever he faced in the desert took everything he had. But the tablet feels significant. Heavy. Wrong.&r&r
You look east. The dunes stretch endlessly. Something out there broke that man.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest3A77039D6CAB3: &o&8He is slow — four centuries of immobility will do that — but he is &bimmensely strong&r. And the throne feeds him. Every time you push him back, the amber light flares and his wounds close.&r&r
You realise quickly: you cannot kill him while he touches the throne.
&4You must break the throne first. Then him.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest3AF6284A78CE0: You find a dead crow on your path, pinned to a tree with a silver needle.
A small note tied to its leg reads:
&5&o"The pact is broken. Come to the &2Hawthorn Circle&r&5&o before the next full moon, or the curse spreads. You know what you did."&r&r
&o&8You don't remember making any pact. But you notice, with some unease, that you can no longer smell anything. The curse has already begun.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest3F0CA1B27BA42: Inside the Desert Temple, beneath the false floor and past the TNT trap, you find an antechamber that doesn't appear on any map.
The walls are lined with amber panels, and at the room's centre sits a stone plinth where something once rested — recently removed.
&o&8The tablet in your pack vibrates. You pull it out and the glyphs are glowing faintly. Someone moved the Amber Throne recently — and whatever seal kept it dormant left with it.&r&r
A second inscription on the plinth, in the common tongue — scratched hurriedly by someone after the ancient builders:
&4&o"Do not seek the Throne. Do not approach the Throne. Bury this again."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest43786F9835A6F: Maren needs three ritual components to complete the binding:
A &dBlossom of the Corrupted&r — found only in places where the spirit has already passed through.
The &aHeart of a Treant&r — because the binding requires something ancient that remembers the forest before the corruption.
&5Witch's Brew&r — the catalyst that ties the components together.

&o&8"The spirit feeds on fear and neglect," Maren explains, grinding something foul-smelling in a stone mortar. "It has been growing for years, unseen. We must act while it's still tethered to this forest."&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest47DDA9FE7F251: At the base of the shaft, past a collapsed barricade, you find what the mineshaft was unknowingly built on top of:

&o&8Not a ruin — or not entirely. The buildings are intact. The streets are swept. The forges are cold but their tools are left neatly on their hooks, as if the smiths stepped away for lunch and simply never came back. There are no bodies. No signs of violence. Just absence.&r&r
A notice board near the settlement's entrance still holds a single posting, the words pressed deep into a copper plate:
&6&o"BY ORDER OF THE FORGE-MASTERS: All hands to the Deep Works. Bring nothing. Leave nothing. The Vein must be sealed."&r&r
&o&8It is not dated. The dwarves did not track time the way surface-folk do.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest4CC7C1C1BFE86: Each animal and human being can now bleed. This is an effect that deals damage similar to poison and can be removed using a new item: Bandages. The chance of bleeding is lower the more pieces of armor you have.
Bleeding is caused by many different sources such as receiving sword damage, being bitten by a spider, or by touching a cactus...
It is important to keep bandages with you at all times because bleeding becomes more deadly as the game progresses!
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest4E816C3883966: Dual Wielding allows you to use a weapon from your offhand. Parry This! uses a unique implementation of dual wield. Instead of using right click to trigger an offhand attack, you will alternate between your main and off hands by attacking normally. Dual wielding grants a small boost in attack speed, as well as a penalty for using weapons with differing speeds. Some weapons are marked as "two handed", and cannot be used for dual wielding.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest5005C51CA1B68: Backstab is a powerful special attack that can be performed by attacking a target from behind. It's hard to hit, but if you get your aim right, you can deal 300%% damage to your target. Combined with vanilla stealth mechanics, such as sneaking and invisibility, a rouge or ninja playstyle becomes practical. In pvp, backstabs are practically impossible if your enemy knows you are coming, so use stealth to your advantage. Backstab works with both melee and ranged attacks, making sniping possible as well. Backstab only affects targets with 20 max health or less by default.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest529B2EAA80671: &ocommand tooltip: adds an ability to locate trash&r
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest52FEA7C8BC5C3: With the corrupted creatures slain and the components gathered, Maren performs the ritual at the Hawthorn Circle.
&2The fire burns green, then white.&r The carved symbols on the stones glow and fade. You feel something leave you — a cold, clammy presence that you hadn't noticed until it was gone.
&o&8Maren stands and brushes ash from her coat. "The curse is lifted. The spirit is bound." She reaches into her cloak and produces a small vial — deep green, swirling with something alive.&r&r
The forest smells like pine again.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest5300718DDDE89: You turn to find a woman watching you from the tree line — old, sharp-eyed, wrapped in layers of dried herbs and dark cloth. She doesn't look surprised to see you.
&5&o"So the curse found its mark. Good. That means the ritual worked — at least in part."&r&r

&o&8She introduces herself as &5Maren&r&o&8, a hedge-witch who has been tracking a corrupted nature spirit for months. She needed an &2anchor&r for her binding ritual — a living person whose name the forest could attach itself to. She chose you, a stranger passing through.&r&r
"I apologise for the inconvenience," she says, not apologetically at all.
"Help me finish the ritual and the curse lifts. Refuse and... it won't."
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest533001DF566AB: This pack uses the 1.18 features to create brand new terrain in the Nether - with 3D biomes and tons of new terrain types.
It also brings back the Nether mountains from the original Nether Update trailer. Many players saw these and were upset that they weren't in the game - we've added them for you to enjoy!

3D biomes are used extensively as well - you might find a different biome at the base of a mountain than at the top, and you'll find much more biome variation in normal Nether terrain. Biome borders that were previously impossible are now commonplace - any biome can border any other biome!
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest549BD266B80D0: Without Maren's guidance, you have only one option: find the corrupted spirit yourself and destroy it before it destroys you.

&o&8The curse feeds on inaction. Every hour you spend not moving, you can feel it tightening — a slow numbness creeping from your fingers upward. You've lost smell. You're losing colour. Soon, you won't feel the cold.&r&r

You need to understand what you're facing. The forest holds traces of the spirit's path — &4blackened bark&r, &5withered flowers&r, &0soil that crumbles to ash&r. Follow the damage and you'll find the source.
Maren called it a &4corrupted nature spirit&r. That means it was once something else. Something that remembers being alive.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest56742958BEAAE: The door does not open with a groan or a crash. It dissolves — the stone pulling itself apart in perfect silence, each block receding into the wall as if it was always meant to be there.

&o&8Beyond it is not a chamber. It is a &4vein of crystal&r — a seam running through the rock from floor to ceiling, descending at an angle into the earth for as far as your torch can illuminate. The crystals are the colour of deep amber, pulsing with a slow rhythmic light. Like breathing.&r&r

&o&8It is not malevolent. Not welcoming. Simply present — enormous and ancient and indifferent in the way mountains are indifferent. But it notices you. A gentle pressure. A warmth. A sense of being observed by something that has no concept of time or danger or consequence.&r&r
You think you understand now why the dwarves stayed.
You think you understand why the Forge-Masters sealed it.
&4You are not sure those two things are compatible.
Stand before the Vein in silence

ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest678EBDCA589C3: You find the buried treasure chest on a nearby shore.

ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest71246B33DA11E: You look at the sleeping dwarves. You look at the door.

You turn around and begin the long climb back to the surface.
&o&8Behind you, the hum changes pitch — almost imperceptibly. The Vein goes a little quieter.&r&r
ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest75649EE009DB7: The trail leads you to a place the map shows as empty — a hollow in the forest floor ringed by dead oaks, their roots knotted together overhead into something that resembles a ribcage.

ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest786812927C83E: Below the waterline, half-buried in silt and barnacles, you find the captain's quarters.

ftbquests.chapter.wip.quest7BD97790AFE58: Certain weapons have a new attribute called "armor penetration". This attribute specifies a percentage of the weapon's damage that ignores armor. So if your weapon has 35%% armor penetration, 35%% of the weapon's damage will ignore armor. All bludgeoning weapons possess armor penetration to varying degrees.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.questA2B64BF181825: &9The Ocean Monument&r looms from the dark water like a forgotten cathedral.
Its stone corridors are patrolled by Guardians — ancient constructs that seem almost... deliberate. As if placed here to protect something specific.

&o&8You push deeper, past the Elder Guardian's territory, into the monument's flooded heart. And there — wedged between two prismarine pillars — is a chest bearing the privateer's emblem. A compass rose, surrounded by a serpent biting its own tail.&r&r
The chest is sealed with an unusual lock.
&4Salazar hasn't opened it yet. You arrived in time.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.questA8694E6D7E5B9: You can choose starter gear in the Specialization chapter. These are just items, there are no other benefits.
You only get to choose one path, and get three sets of rewards total.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.questA9AB758ADFBDE: You search the settlement for clues about where everyone went.

The homes are tidy but personal — a child's toy left on a windowsill, a half-knitted sock on a chair, a pot still hanging over a cold hearth. These weren't soldiers following an evacuation order. These were families.

&o&8In the settlement's small library you find a journal. Most of it is mundane: supply orders, shift rotations, complaints about the water pressure in the lower forges. But the final entry is different in tone:&r&r

&6&o"The Vein sang again last night. Thrumvor says it's getting louder. He won't tell us what it's saying. He says we wouldn't want to know. We leave at first bell. The Deep Works await. Ancestors guide our hands."&r&r
&o&8The Vein. Every mention of the dwarves' collapse references it — something they found deep underground that sang. Something that required a seal.&r&r
You are going to need supplies before you follow them down.
Pack torches for the deep dark
Read the Journal — Prepare to Descend
ftbquests.chapter.wip.questC2E1DD15D80D4: Parrying is a relatively simple mechanic. When you are hit with a melee attack, if you are swinging a weapon the attack will be deflected. A "weapon" in this case is any item with an attack damage value, eg a sword or pickaxe. To parry, you must be aiming at the attacker. Parrying is easier the higher the attack recharge meter is. A successful parry reduces the defense meter.
ftbquests.chapter.wip.questE3ED5CB4D7FDB: It is enormous. Carved from a single piece of amber the size of a house, its surface cracked with age but still pulsing with a dim inner light.
&4Seated upon it — or rather, fused with it — is a figure that was once human. The ancient king.&r

&o&8He did not die on his throne. He became it. The amber preserves him, but the preservation has become something else — a prison, and a power source for whatever has been restlessly stirring beneath this desert for centuries.&r&r
"&6&oYou carry the seal-tablet. You broke the seal. You&r&4&o are responsible.&r&r"
